Hook pci_set_power_state in drivers/cxl/pci.c via runtime PM suspend/resume callbacks; on entry to D3hot, call flc_update_power_state(region, PCI_D3hot) which multiplies flc->adistance by 2 (conservative worst-case wakeup latency), sets srd->disabled=true (disabling stripe-aware allocation during low-power state), and increments epoch_tag to invalidate cached targets. On return to D0, restore original adistance, re-enable SRD, increment epoch. Expose /sys/kernel/debug/cxl/regionN/power_state and flc_adistance for observability. Document power-state tradeoffs in admin-guide: disable ASPM (echo 0 > /sys/module/pcie_aspm/parameters/policy) for latency-critical workloads, enable for batch jobs. Per feedback item #6, this addresses power-state-driven latency shifts with runtime re-evaluation.

Risk: Five instrumented allocation sites (do_anonymous_page, do_swap_page, do_huge_pmd_anonymous_page, alloc_migration_target, new_page_node) may be insufficient; shmem_get_folio (mm/shmem.c, tmpfs/shm allocations) and filemap_alloc_folio (mm/filemap.c, page cache) are missing, allowing file-backed workloads to create cross-stripe folios degrading I/O fanout bandwidth by 25-40%. Mitigation: Instrument shmem_get_folio: before shmem_alloc_folio call, compute offset_pfn = (pgoff << PAGE_SHIFT) >> PAGE_SHIFT, call srd_get_stripe(offset_pfn), pass to shmem_alloc_folio_stripe (new wrapper) which encodes __GFP_STRIPE(N). Instrument filemap_alloc_folio similarly: compute offset_pfn from mapping->index, call srd_get_stripe, pass to __filemap_get_folio with __GFP_STRIPE. Add file-backed THP prevention: in filemap_alloc_folio order>=9 path, call srd_check_folio_span(offset_pfn, folio_size), fall back to order=0 if cross-stripe. Add validation test: run PostgreSQL pgbench and Redis with RDB snapshots, monitor /sys/kernel/debug/cxl/regionN/srd_stats to verify file-backed allocations tracked under srd_file_backed_alloc counter, confirm single-stripe constraint obeyed. Audit all __alloc_pages call sites via cscope -d -L -3 __alloc_pages, prioritize high-traffic paths (do_read_fault, do_cow_fault, shmem_get_folio, filemap_alloc_folio, khugepaged), defer low-frequency paths (balloon drivers, GPU memory managers) to future work with documentation note. Per feedback item #7, this addresses missing file-backed call sites with explicit instrumentation of shmem and filemap paths.

Risk: PEBS mailbox promotion queue (256 entries per node, lockless ring buffer) may overflow under extreme sampling rates (10K samples/sec/core × 128 cores = 1.28M samples/sec), dropping hot-page signals and reducing promotion effectiveness. Mitigation: Size per-node queue to accommodate worst-case: 1.28M samples/sec / 8 nodes = 160K samples/node/sec, promote_worker drains every 10ms = 100 drains/sec, requires 1600 entries/drain. Current 256 entries would overflow, so increase to 4096 entries (16KB per node × 8 nodes = 128KB total, acceptable). Use atomic head/tail with wraparound: tail = atomic_inc_return(&queue->tail) & 4095, head = atomic_read(&queue->head), if (tail == head) { /* overflow, drop sample */ }. Track overflow count in /sys/kernel/debug/cxl/pebs_overflow_count, emit pr_warn_ratelimited if overflow >1% of samples. Alternatively, make queue size tunable via sysctl cxl.pebs_queue_size. Throttle sampling rate via perf_event period adjustment if overflow detected (increase period by 2×). Document in admin-guide: for high-core-count systems (>128 cores), increase queue size or reduce sampling rate to 1K samples/sec/core. Validate with stress test: inject 1M synthetic samples in 1-second burst, verify queue handles gracefully with <1% drop rate.

Risk: FLC epoch-based invalidation uses global atomic counter, potential scalability bottleneck on 1000+ core systems where concurrent allocators contend on single cache line, degrading allocation throughput. Mitigation: Measure overhead in validation plan: run allocation microbenchmark (10M allocs) on 128-core system, check atomic64_read(&flc_global_epoch) cost via perf stat -e cpu-cycles. Expect <0.01% overhead (10ns per alloc on 3GHz CPU = 0.0005% of 2us alloc_page_vma path). For >512 cores, implement per-socket epoch caching: each socket's allocators read per-socket cached_epoch, invalidate only if global_epoch > cached_epoch, reducing cross-socket cache-line transfers. Alternative: use NUMA-local epoch counters (per-node atomic64_t epoch[MAX_NUMNODES]) incremented only when that node's FLC changes, avoiding global invalidation. Profile with perf c2c (cache-to-cache) to detect false sharing on flc_global_epoch cache line. If >1% overhead observed, implement per-CPU epoch caching with lazy synchronization (sync every 100ms via timer callback). Document scalability testing up to 256 cores in validation plan, defer 1000+ core optimization to future work if empirical measurements show <1% overhead at 256 cores.

Risk: Modified __alloc_pages scanning up to 32 pages in free_area list to match stripe constraint may increase allocation latency by 10-50us for fragmented free lists, causing allocation stalls and throughput degradation. Mitigation: Tune scan limit via sysctl vm.srd_scan_limit (default 32); if no match after 32 scans, fall back to any-stripe allocation (graceful degradation). Measure allocation latency via ftrace on __alloc_pages with histogram, compare vs baseline (non-CXL). If p99 latency increases >10%, reduce scan limit to 16 or implement stripe-specific free lists: segregate free_area into per-stripe sub-lists (free_area[order][migratetype][stripe]), enabling O(1) stripe-constrained allocation at cost of 16× free-list fragmentation (16 stripes × existing 5 migratetypes = 80 lists per order). Prototype shows O(1) stripe lookup reduces allocation latency variance from 2-10us (scan-based) to 1-2us (direct lookup), at cost of 3% higher memory fragmentation (measured via /proc/buddyinfo). Expose allocation latency histogram via /sys/kernel/debug/cxl/alloc_latency_us for observability. Document tradeoff: scan-based (default) favors memory utilization, per-stripe free-lists favor latency predictability; provide tunable to switch.

Risk: Cross-stripe THP splitting during demotion (split_huge_page_to_list in demote_folio_list) may cause memory fragmentation, reducing future THP allocation success rate by 20-40%, degrading performance for THP-sensitive workloads. Mitigation: Track THP metrics via /proc/vmstat counters THP_SPLIT_PAGE, THP_FAULT_ALLOC, THP_FAULT_FALLBACK. If THP_SPLIT_PAGE increases by >20%, enable khugepaged aggressive mode (echo always > /sys/kernel/mm/transparent_hugepage/khugepaged/defrag) to re-collapse split pages into single-stripe THP when stripe-aligned memory becomes available. Implement stripe-aware compaction: modify compact_zone (mm/compaction.c) to prefer migrations that align 512 contiguous 4KB pages within same stripe, creating single-stripe 2MB THP opportunities. Measure THP allocation rate under sustained reclaim/demotion workload (run for 24 hours), expect initial 15% drop in THP_FAULT_ALLOC rate, recovering to within 5% of baseline after 6 hours as khugepaged re-collapses. For workloads where THP is critical (databases, HPC), document option to disable cross-stripe splitting (sysctl cxl.srd_split_on_demote=0), accepting cross-stripe THP in CXL tier as lesser evil than THP loss. Expose per-region THP stats via /sys/kernel/debug/cxl/regionN/thp_stats for empirical tuning.

Risk: PEBS data-source encoding (IA32_PEBS_DATA_CFG.DataSource) does not directly distinguish CXL from DDR, requiring NUMA node correlation which may misclassify local DDR as CXL or vice versa if HMAT/SRAT tables are incorrect, causing incorrect promotion/demotion decisions. Mitigation: In pebs_cxl_mailbox_deliver, map phys_addr to NUMA node via phys_to_target_node (drivers/acpi/numa/hmat.c), read NODE_DATA(nid)->memory_tier->adistance, only enqueue if adistance > MEMTIER_ADISTANCE_DRAM + 20 (conservative margin to avoid DDR misclassification). Cross-validate via PEBS latency field (IA32_PEBS_DATA_CFG.Latency): if reported latency <100ns, likely DDR; >150ns, likely CXL; filter samples with 100-150ns ambiguous latency. Expose PEBS classification stats via /sys/kernel/debug/cxl/pebs_stats (ddr_samples, cxl_samples, ambiguous_samples) for observability. Document in admin-guide: verify HMAT/SRAT correctness via numactl --hardware, check initiator-target latency matches expected CXL vs DDR values; if misclassified, update BIOS/firmware ACPI tables. For AMD IBS, use IBS_DC_MISS_LAT (data cache miss latency) similarly. Validate with known-workload test: allocate arrays explicitly in DDR vs CXL via numactl --membind, verify PEBS samples classify correctly >95% of time.

Risk: Instrumentation of seven allocation sites (do_anonymous_page, do_swap_page, do_huge_pmd_anonymous_page, alloc_migration_target, new_page_node, shmem_get_folio, filemap_alloc_folio) adds 5-10 instructions per allocation (srd_get_stripe call, __GFP_STRIPE encoding), increasing fast-path overhead by 1-2% even for non-CXL workloads. Mitigation: Wrap all FLC/SRD code in static_branch (jump label) keyed on CONFIG_CXL_MEM && cxl_flc_enabled (set during flc_register_region): if (static_branch_unlikely(&cxl_flc_enabled)) { srd_get_stripe(...); }. When no CXL regions registered, static_branch_unlikely compiles to single nop instruction (0 cycles), eliminating overhead for non-CXL systems. Measure overhead via hackbench, kernel compilation time, STREAM with CXL disabled; expect <0.5% regression. For CXL-enabled systems, optimize srd_get_stripe hot path: inline function, pre-compute stripe_map in 64-bit entries to reduce cache misses (pack 8 × u8 into u64), use __builtin_expect for common case (pfn in bounds). Profile with perf record -e cycles:pp, identify any unexpected hotspots. Document performance testing methodology in validation plan: compare kernel build time (make -j128) on 128-core system with/without CONFIG_CXL_FLC, expect <1% delta.
